request.question: Which rock group has more band members in its core group, White Magic or Pale Saints?

title: Pale Saints
La Mar Enfortuna (band)
Mormon folklore
Opus Pro
White Rose
White Witch (band)
Edsel Auctioneer
List of the Doobie Brothers band members
White Magic (band)
Slow Buildings
sentences: Pale Saints were a British alternative rock band formed in 1987 by Ian Masters (bass/vocals), Graeme Naysmith (guitar), and Chris Cooper (drums) in Leeds, England.
The group began as a jangly indie pop band, influenced by Primal Scream's early sound.
La Mar Enfortuna is the Sephardic side project from the alt rock group Elysian Fields, Oren Bloedow and Jennifer Charles.

La Mar Enfortuna is a modern interpretation of lost or forgotten music, mostly of the Sephardim, from the 11th to the 16th century, with songs sung in Ladino, Arabic, Aramaic, Spanish, Greek, and English.
They incorporate the sounds of jazz, folk, rock, Middle Eastern, and Latin musics.
Besides Charles and Bloedow, the core group as it now stands includes Doug Wieselman, Ted Reichman, Robert DiPietro, and Brahim Fribgane.
The group released two albums, an eponymous album in 2001 and "Convivencia" in 2007, both on John Zorn's Tzadik label.
Guests on "Convivencia" include Ljova Zhurbin, Chuscales, Liaqat Khan, and Benjamin Lapidus.
The band has played in Washington, DC, New York City, at La Cigale in Paris, La Huerta de Federico Garcia Lorca in Granada, Spain, and the Chicago World Music Festival.
Mormon folklore is a body of expressive culture unique to The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (LDS Church) and its members.
Mormon folklore includes tales, oral history, popular beliefs, customs, music, jokes, and material culture traditions.
In folklore studies, Mormons can be seen as a regional group, since the core group of Mormon settlers in Utah had a common religion and had to modify their surroundings for survival.
Opus Pro is a Latvian heavy rock group, established in 1986.
The core group consists of lead singer Oleg Andreyev (Alex), and guitarist and songwriter Harry Zarins.
Opus Pro was one of the most popular Latvian groups in the 1980s and 1990s.
The band released two original albums "Dzimene" and "I'm going to steal you" and a "Greatest Hits".
The White Rose (German: "die Weiße Rose" ) was a non-violent, intellectual resistance group in Nazi Germany led by a group of students and a professor at the University of Munich.
The group conducted an anonymous leaflet and graffiti campaign which called for active opposition to the Nazi regime.
Their activities started in Munich on June 27th, 1942, and ended with the arrest of the core group by the Gestapo on February 18th, 1943.

They, as well as other members and supporters of the group who carried on distributing the pamphlets, faced show trials by the Nazi People's Court ("Volksgerichtshof"), and many of them were sentenced to death or imprisonment.
White Witch was a glam rock/psychedelic/hard rock band from Tampa, Florida that made two albums for Capricorn Records in the early 1970s.
Their name was a paean to "white magic", contrary to the "black magic" of groups like Black Sabbath.

As the band announced before their shows: "To bring good where there once was evil, to bring love where there once was hate, to bring wisdom where there once was ignorance; this is the power of White Witch".
The Edsel Auctioneer were a band formed in Leeds in 1988 by Ashley Horner (guitar/ vocals), Phil Pettler (bass/ vocals), Aidan Winterburn (vocals, guitar) and Chris Cooper (drums).
They were named after the ill-fated Ford automobile whose front grille was supposed to have resembled a woman's pudenda.
Best friends with Pale Saints, they lived on the same street in Leeds, Harold Avenue (which spawned the so-called Voice of the Harolds).
The American band The Doobie Brothers has had a plethora of members since its inception in 1970, including guitarist/singer Tom Johnston and keyboardist/singer Michael McDonald.
The Doobie Brothers employ official members and a small group of touring musicians, usually with a core group shown in promotional material.
Guitarist/singer Patrick Simmons is the only constant member of the group, and the only one who has appeared on all of their releases.
White Magic is a psychedelic folk rock group formed in Brooklyn, New York City, and led by singer/guitarist/pianist/composer Mira Billotte.
Billotte performs under this moniker both with accompaniment or solo, using a daf, shruti box, and singing a cappella.
She is a modern trobairitz and, invoking both traditional and experimental folk, White Magic's sound ranges from loud psychedelia to meditative trance.
Slow Buildings is the third and final studio album from Pale Saints, released by 4AD on 29 August 1994.

This was the band's first album following the departure of original singer and bassist Ian Masters and addition of Colleen Browne on bass/vocals; guitarist Meriel Barham, who had joined Pale Saints for their previous album "In Ribbons", also took over lead vocal duties.
"Slow Buildings" features the singles "Fine Friend", "Henry", "One Blue Hill", and "Angel (Will You Be My)", with the latter three songs released as radio singles only.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

The project signed to Western Vinyl in 2008, followed by the five-piece group's signing to Secretly Canadian in 2009.

Pale Saints | Pale Saints were a British alternative rock band formed in 1987 by Ian Masters (bass/vocals), Graeme Naysmith (guitar), and Chris Cooper (drums) in Leeds, England.  The group began as a jangly indie pop band, influenced by Primal Scream's early sound.

As the band announced before their shows: "To bring good where there once was evil, to bring love where there once was hate, to bring wisdom where there once was ignorance; this is the power of White Witch".

Principal Edwards Magic Theatre | Principal Edwards Magic Theatre was an English performance art collective in the United Kingdom made up of about 14 musicians, poets, dancers, and sound and lighting technicians.  It existed between 1968 and 1971, after which core members formed a more conventional rock band under the shortened name Principal Edwards.

Damn (band) | Damn!  is a funk-rock band based in Lund, Sweden.  The four core members are joined on stage and on record by a collective of DJs and live musicians.  The group's first album "Natural Sounds" was recorded by the group as a trio and released on Lund Records in Sweden and LoveCat Music in North America.  The band members are also videographers and direct their own videos.  The band has toured Japan, Europe and North America.

The Doobie Brothers employ official members and a small group of touring musicians, usually with a core group shown in promotional material.  Guitarist/singer Patrick Simmons is the only constant member of the group, and the only one who has appeared on all of their releases.

Billotte performs under this moniker both with accompaniment or solo, using a daf, shruti box, and singing a cappella.  She is a modern trobairitz and, invoking both traditional and experimental folk, White Magic's sound ranges from loud psychedelia to meditative trance.

response.summary_1: Evidence: Pale Saints were "formed ... by Ian Masters (bass/vocals), Graeme Naysmith (guitar), and Chris Cooper (drums)" — three core members. White Magic is "led by singer/guitarist/pianist/composer Mira Billotte" and "Billotte performs under this moniker both with accompaniment or solo," but the passages do not state a core-group size for White Magic. Missing / next hop: retrieve a personnel or "members"/"core members" listing for White Magic (e.g., the White Magic article or band lineup) to confirm whether Mira Billotte is the sole core member or if the project has multiple core members.
response.hop2_query: White Magic Mira Billotte members lineup core members personnel
hop2_docs: White Magic (band) | White Magic is a psychedelic folk rock group formed in Brooklyn, New York City, and led by singer/guitarist/pianist/composer Mira Billotte.  Billotte performs under this moniker both with accompaniment or solo, using a daf, shruti box, and singing a cappella.  She is a modern trobairitz and, invoking both traditional and experimental folk, White Magic's sound ranges from loud psychedelia to meditative trance.

Mira, Mirror | Mira, Mirror is a young adult fantasy novel written by Mette Ivie Harrison.  The novel was first published in 2004.  The story of the novel is told from the viewpoint of the magic mirror from the fairy tale "Snow White".  "Mira" is a main character.

Mira Ricardel | Mira Ricardel is an American government official who currently serves as Under Secretary of Commerce for Export Administration.  Prior to assuming her current role, she served as Special Assistant to President Donald Trump and Associate Director for Presidential Personnel.  She is a member of the Council on Foreign Relations.

Final Conflict | Final Conflict is a hardcore punk band from Long Beach, California formed in 1983.  They have gone through various lineup changes since with the only constant member being their original guitar player, Jeff Harp.  Currently the band's lineup includes 3/4 of the Original 1988 "Ashes to Ashes" Record lineup, Ron Martinez (Vocals), Jeff Harp (Guitar), Warren Renfrow (Bass), Drummer Nick Manning are the current core lineup.  Shane McLachlan of the grindcore band Phobia is an active member as replacement bassist.
List of Split Enz members | This is a list of personnel from the various lineups of Split Enz's studio and touring bands.

List of the Cure band members | The following is the history of The Cure's personnel and various lineups from 1976 to 2012, going back to the English rock group's origins as Malice and Easy Cure, and also including guest musicians, studio personnel, and side projects.

Kings of Chaos (band) | Kings of Chaos are an international band featuring a core lineup of Guns N' Roses and Velvet Revolver members Matt Sorum, Duff McKagan, and Gilby Clarke as well as a revolving lineup of members of Def Leppard, Deep Purple, Aerosmith and more.  The group plays songs from all of these bands, and other classic rock covers.  To date, they have only recorded and released one song, "Never Before", on the Deep Purple tribute, "".
hop2_titles: List of the Cure band members
response.summary_2: Pale Saints have more core members: Pale Saints were formed by three core members, while White Magic is essentially a project led by a single primary member, Mira Billotte (who performs solo or with accompanists).
